Article Text: Raghav Chadha vs Aam Aadmi Party: Simmering rift now out in open; what's next for AAP MP

Article Title: 'People not ready to vote for you': Kanimozhi targets NDA during campaign in Tamil Nadu's Madurai
Article Text: 'People not ready to vote for you': Kanimozhi targets NDA during campaign in Tamil Nadu's Madurai
Article Summary: Kanimozhi targets NDA during campaign in Tamil Nadu's Madurai. "People not ready to vote for you," she tells NDA leaders. "We are here to make Tamil Nadu a better state for all," she says. "I am the voice of the people," she adds.

Article Title: 'He should resign first': Mamata targets PM Modi over voter list row in Bengal polls
Article Text:
CM Mamata Banerjee

Watch After SC Rap On Malda Hostage Row, Mamata Banerjee Claims BJP Wants President’s Rule In Bengal After SC Rap On Malda Hostage Row, Mamata Banerjee Claims BJP Wants President’s Rule In Bengal

Poll What are your views on the conduct of elections in West Bengal? Fair and transparent. Fraudulent and biased.

NEW DELHI: West Bengal chief minister Mamata Banerjee on Friday launched a scathing attack on Prime Minister Narendra Modi , alleging that he should resign first if voter lists contained names of infiltrators.Speaking at a rally in Dakshin Dinajpur, she said: "If voter list contained names of infiltrators, Modi also won with their votes earlier, so he should resign first."She questioned the allegations around electoral rolls and asked why the same voter lists used in the 2024 general elections are now being challenged ahead of the assembly polls.The chief minister also addressed the recent incident in Malda district, where judicial officers were gheraoed for several hours.She blamed the All India Majlis-e-Ittehadul Muslimeen (AIMIM) and the Indian Secular Front (ISF) for the incident, while accusing the Congress and the BJP of instigation.Providing details, Banerjee said the main accused in the case had been arrested."The BJP loaned him (the accused) from the AIMIM and brought him here," she alleged while addressing the rally at Harirampur.She further added: "ISF is with them; Congress and BJP have also provided instigation."Banerjee said the Criminal Investigation Department (CID) arrested the main accused, advocate Mofakkarul Islam from Bagdogra airport while he was trying to flee."It is they who engineered the violence in Malda's Mothabari," she said.Banerjee also raised concerns over the conduct of the elections, alleging that officials were transferred to facilitate the movement of outsiders into the state.She further claimed irregularities involving security forces."Money is being brought in using CRPF vehicles. I have a record of these movements and will expose them at the appropriate time," she alleged.The Trinamool Congress chief also targeted home minister Amit Shah, who has announced plans to stay in West Bengal during the election period.Banerjee claimed that his presence would not benefit the BJP electorally. She said that Shah’s extended stay in the state during the polls would instead lead to a reduction in the party’s vote share.
Article Summary: West Bengal chief minister Mamata Banerjee on Friday launched a scathing attack on Prime Minister Narendra Modi. She said if voter lists contained names of infiltrators, Modi also won with their votes earlier, so he should resign first. She questioned the allegations around electoral rolls and asked why the same voter lists used in 2024 general elections are now being challenged ahead of the assembly polls.

Article Title: Khushbaz Singh Jatana Accident: Stuck on highway, Punjab Congress leader Khushbaz Singh Jatana, driver killed as truck slams Defender
Article Text:
By: Manoj Dhaka

Poll What do you think should be prioritized more regarding road safety? Strict enforcement of traffic rules Better road infrastructure and awareness





SONIPAT: Punjab Congress functionary Khushbaz Singh Jatana and his driver were killed, while his gunman was critically injured after a speeding truck rammed into their stationary Land Rover Defender SUV on the Kundli-Manesar-Palwal (KMP) Expressway near Sonipat on Thursday evening.Jatana had contested the 2017 and 2022 Punjab assembly elections from Talwandi Sabo as a Congress candidate, but lost both times.According to police, the accident took place at 6.15pm near the Jakhauli toll plaza. Jatana was travelling from Delhi to Punjab in a Land Rover Defender SUV when the vehicle reportedly developed a tyre puncture.Police said the SUV had been pulled over to the roadside and a local mechanic had been called to replace the tyre. While the driver and mechanic were changing the wheel, Jatana and his gunman were standing near the vehicle when a speeding truck allegedly hit the SUV from behind.The impact was severe, and Jatana and the driver died on the spot, police said. The gunman sustained critical injuries, while the mechanic was also hurt.The injured were rushed to Sonipat civil hospital, from where the gunman was referred to Rohtak for advanced treatment.The mechanic is undergoing treatment.Rai police station SHO Shetty Malik said the truck driver fled the spot with the vehicle. Police have identified the truck number, registered a case and launched a manhunt. CCTV footage from nearby areas is also being scanned. The bodies have been sent for postmortem.
Article Summary: Punjab Congress functionary Khushbaz Singh Jatana and his driver were killed. His gunman was critically injured after a speeding truck rammed into their stationary Land Rover Defender SUV. Jatana had contested the 2017 and 2022 Punjab assembly elections from Talwandi Sabo as a Congress candidate.
